title: Analyze, improve, and deploy ziontechgroup app
body: Fix build and static generation errors by simplifying problematic pages and correcting component imports/exports and configuration. The project was failing to build and generate static pages due to a combination of issues: malformed JSX in several pages, incorrect component import paths, missing exports in some UI components, and a PostCSS configuration that conflicted with the project's ES module setup. Additionally, many pages were overly complex, relying on non-existent components or data, leading to static generation failures. This PR simplifies these pages to allow the application to build and run successfully. ---

title: Website audit and enhancement
body: Fix broken links, create missing content, and improve site navigation to enable a successful build of the core Zion Tech Group website. Initial analysis revealed numerous build errors, malformed JSX, and missing component definitions across the project. Several non-essential showcase and pricing pages with complex data dependencies were temporarily disabled to achieve a clean build and focus on the core site functionality. ---

title: Analyze, improve, and deploy ziontechgroup app
body: Implement comprehensive website improvements to enhance performance, user experience, and modern design, addressing identified code quality and architectural issues. ---

title: Expand services and deploy updates
body: Adds new diversified micro-SaaS, IT, and AI services with a new landing page, and resolves merge conflicts by preferring TypeScript component files. Merge conflicts were resolved by removing duplicate JavaScript component files (e.g., `HireNowCTA.js`, `Button.js`) in favor of their existing TypeScript counterparts. Minor conflict markers in `src/components/ui/badge.tsx` and `tsconfig.json` were also addressed to ensure a clean build. ---

title: Website audit and enhancement
body: Adds missing pages to resolve broken links and implements a new site layout with improved header, footer, and sidebar navigation. Existing redirects in `next.config.js` and `netlify.toml` pointed to non-existent pages, causing 404 errors. This PR creates the necessary target pages and integrates them into a new, organized site layout with a shared header, footer, and sidebar. ---
